forecasts.reasoning: The border closure was initiated by Benin in early May 2025 in response to the emergence of the 'Mahmuda' terror group and allegations of arms smuggling and illegal agricultural exports. Far from being a temporary measure, the blockade was expanded to include Lagos land borders and Ogun waterway routes as recently as May 21, 2025. Security-related border closures in this region typically involve complex, multi-month military operations and diplomatic negotiations. With the situation still escalating in late May and no reports of a breakthrough or joint security resolution, it is highly improbable that Benin will reverse this 'national security strategy' and reopen the Kwara border crossings by the July 1, 2025 deadline.

Extensive evidence from multiple reliable sources (Politico, The Guardian, The National) confirms a high-level diplomatic conference is scheduled for June 17-20, 2025, in New York, explicitly designed to facilitate the recognition of a Palestinian state. Major UN member countries currently not recognizing Palestine—including France, the UK, Canada, Belgium, and the Netherlands—have been identified as actively discussing or being 'on the brink' of joint recognition at this event. Despite a mid-May rumor of a possible delay by France, the most recent reports (up to May 31, 2025) reinforce that the conference and the expected recognitions remain on track. Given that only one country needs to follow through to trigger a 'Yes' resolution, and a coordinated group effort is planned, the probability is very high.
